headlineSummary: The day’s sentiment skews negative for Anthropic on limited evidence, while OpenAI and Google Gemini are close together in negative territory and Microsoft Copilot sits nearer neutral with limited evidence [E1][E3][E2].

ranking.summary: Anthropic draws the strongest negative tone in the set, with comments criticizing Claude’s business motives and recent quality/trustworthiness on limited evidence [E1][E3].
Google Gemini is viewed negatively on limited evidence, mainly from a comment grouping Gemini with other profit-driven AI tools [E1].
OpenAI is also viewed negatively on limited evidence, with the cited comment bundling Codex into the same productivity-extraction criticism [E1].
Microsoft Copilot lands in mixed-neutral territory on limited evidence, though the cited comment notes it can worsen enterprise bottlenecks and hurt ROI [E2].

evidence.excerpt: AI/LLMs aren't innovation the way TCP/IP, linux, or postgres were. To be clear: claude/codex/gemini/grok/whatever exist for profit, to squeeze the last drop of productivity out of you until there's nothing left, and then you're disposable (laid off).

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude and portrays it as exploitative and profit-driven at workers' expense.
The comment explicitly names Codex and frames it as existing to extract productivity and make workers disposable.
The comment explicitly names Gemini and condemns it as a profit-driven tool that exploits workers.

evidence.excerpt: In my large enterprise world, AI adoption hasn't made it outside of the development teams - only developers have access to Github Copilot. Code takes 6-12 months to make it from commit to production. Development speed was never the bottleneck; it's all the other processes that take time: infra provisioning, testing, sign-offs, change management, deployment scheduling etc. AI makes these post-development bottlenecks worse. Changes are now piling up at the door waiting to get on a release train. Large enterprises need to learn how to ship software faster if they want to lock in ROI on their token spend. Unshipped code is a liability, not an asset.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt explicitly mentions GitHub Copilot and says the resulting AI-driven changes worsen downstream bottlenecks and create ROI problems.

evidence.excerpt: Given the quality of Claude code lately, I wouldn’t trust them in financial services.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude and says its recent quality is bad enough that the speaker would not trust Anthropic in financial services.
